articles.description: TORONTO — Years after Kenneth Law’s arrest, a father who holds the Ontario man responsible for his son’s death by suicide says little has been done to rein in the online trade of lethal substances that continues to claim lives in Canada and abroad. articles.title: Why these women are fighting for a national action plan on endometriosis
articles.description: The disease impacts about 1 in 10 Canadian women, but due to lack of funding and research, it can take years for individuals to get a diagnosis.

articles.title: Return of assigned seating for most public servants bucks private-sector trend
articles.description: Private workplaces are increasingly shifting to unassigned workstations as the average North American company settles on a three-day per week hybrid model, a CBRE report says.

articles.title: As World Cup nears, intelligence agency warns of increased human trafficking risk
articles.description: OTTAWA — As Canada prepares to host World Cup soccer, the federal financial intelligence agency is warning that major sporting events can heighten the risk of vulnerable people being exploited by human traffickers.
